provenance.source_corpus: Sphagnum moss moisture retention following the re‐vegetation of degraded peatlands

source_spans.text: In the autumn of 1999, ecosystem scale restoration commenced using the standard Canadian restoration technique
S. rubellum capitula density ( /SUB C) for upper Sphagnum samples was significantly higher ( p< 0Ð05) for the restored peatland
Residual moisture content at a soil water pressure of /NUL200 mb (/DC2r) was significantly lower ( p< 0Ð05) for the restored site

Lower samples for the natural and naturally re-vegetated sites had a bulk density of 43 š 5 and 41 š 11 kg m /NUL3, respectively, almost twice as high as the value for lower samples from the restored site
Sphagnum moss has been observed to re-vegetate naturally on the remaining cutover peat following human disturbance
peatland can return to a net sink for CO2 within 3–5 years following Sphagnum re-vegetation
sites characterized by soil moisture>50%, soil water pressure>/NUL100 mb and high water table
